channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Now the FAA says gamers are the answer to its air traffic controller shortage
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has struggled for years to have enough air traffic controllers to address shortages, with the US Government Accountability Office (GAO) saying in January...

item.title: I built a 20KB Motion Engine because Svgator,Rive and Lottie were too heavy for the DOM
item.description: The Problem: The "Black Box" of Web Animation We’ve all been there. You want a high-fidelity animation, so you reach for Lottie or Rive. They look amazing, but they come with a cost: The Weight:...

item.title: Notifee is Archived. Here’s a Maintained, New-Architecture Drop-in Replacement
item.description: In December 2024, Notifee stopped receiving updates. In April 2026, Invertase officially archived the repository. If you shipped a React Native app that depended on Notifee’s advanced Android...

item.title: Next.js ile Geliştirilen Uygulamalarda Yük Dengeleme Yöntemleri
item.description: Next.js ve Yük Dengeleme: Tarihçe ve Gelişim Next.js, 2016 yılında Vercel tarafından tanıtıldı ve o zamandan beri web geliştirme topluluğu tarafından büyük bir ilgiyle karşılandı. 🔗 Devamını Oku 📌...

item.title: The JavaScript Runtime: Fixing the Mental Model
item.description: This article is part of a series on the JavaScript event loop. Most explanations of JavaScript's event loop start with: JavaScript is single-threaded. This statement is technically true but...

item.title: JavaScript Event Loop Series: Building the Event Loop Mental Model from Experiments
item.description: I wrote this series because JavaScript has many "asynchronous" mechanisms (await, setTimeout, Promise, requestAnimationFrame) that look similar but behave very differently. At first, I assumed...

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.description: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

item.title: EasyPollVote [Dev Log #1]
item.description: Welcome to the First DEV LOG! Welcome to my first Dev Log of my full stack application called EasyPollVote (EasyPV)! What is EasyPollVote (EasyPV)? It is an application where the ultimate goal is...

item.title: Reviewing AI Generated Work
item.description: Code review has always been one of the most important practices in engineering. It is where mistakes get caught, where knowledge transfers between team members, where standards get enforced, and...

item.title: Running A Better Table
item.description: If you have read the previous articles in this series, you have the theory. You understand why sprints are breaking down, you know what Shape Up is trying to do differently, you can write a pitch,...

item.title: Managing Expectations in the AI Era
item.description: Here is a conversation I suspect a lot of engineering leaders are having right now, in one form or another. A stakeholder - a product director, a founder, a VP of sales - has read something about...

item.title: Building Modern Laravel APIs: Foundations and Project Structure
item.description: I have built a lot of Laravel APIs over the years. Some of them were good. Some of them were not. The ones that were not good all had something in common - they started without a clear set of...

item.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
item.description: San Francisco police have arrested a 20-year-old man suspected of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's Russian Hill house early Friday morning, The San Francisco Standard...

item.title: How to watch the Artemis II astronauts return to Earth
item.description: The Orion capsule, carrying Artemis II commander Reid Wiseman, pilot Victor Glover, mission specialist Christina Koch, and Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen, is expected to land back to Earth after...

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
item.description: The new AI model is being heralded—and feared—as a hacker’s superweapon. Experts say its arrival is a wake-up call for developers who have long made security an afterthought.

item.title: Microsoft finally lets Windows 11 testers unlock experimental features without ViVeTool
item.description: Microsoft is finally allowing Windows 11 testers to get access to experimental features without having to download a third-party app like ViVeTool. The changes are part of making the Windows...

item.title: My go-to electric screwdriver is on sale for over 50 percent off today
item.description: Fanttik, like Hoto, is quickly becoming a household name. The tool brand’s cordless wares are quiet, capable, and certainly more stylish than anything you’d find at your local Ace Hardware or...

item.title: Amazon Luna axes third-party game purchases
item.description: Amazon Luna announced a big change on Friday that will prevent players from purchasing third-party games and subscriptions. The cloud gaming service will remove previously purchased games on June...

item.title: The Iranian Lego AI video creators credit their virality to ‘heart’
item.description: Donald Trump has spun the recent rescue of a downed airman whose fighter jet was destroyed behind Iranian borders as a resounding success. But the story is very different in one of the many viral,...

item.title: Little Snitch’s software counter surveillance jumps from Mac to Linux
item.description: The popular macOS app Little Snitch brought its network-monitoring tools over to Linux this week. In a blog post announcing the launch, one of the developers at Objective Development shared some...

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.description: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.

item.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
item.description: SusHi Tech 2026 is zeroing in on four technology domains reshaping society: AI, Robotics, Resilience, and Entertainment. Expect live demos of humanoid robots, sessions on autonomous driving's...

item.title: Suspect Arrested for Allegedly Throwing Molotov Cocktail at Sam Altman’s Home
item.description: An attacker allegedly threw a molotov cocktail at the OpenAI CEO’s residence before making threats outside of the startup’s headquarters.

item.title: This Startup Wants You to Pay Up to Talk With AI Versions of Human Experts
item.description: Onix is launching a “Substack of bots,” where digital twins of health and wellness influencers dispense advice 24/7. And maybe hawk their products.

item.title: Congress can finally close a mass surveillance loophole — but will they?
item.description: A warrantless wiretapping authority that has facilitated surveillance for decades is up for renewal in Congress. Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA), last reauthorized...

item.title: Bose’s rugged Soundlink Flex Bluetooth speaker is on sale for up to $50 off
item.description: We saw a host of excellent Bose discounts during Amazon’s so-called Big Spring Sale last month; however, none of them were quite as steep as the current price drop we’re seeing on the latest Bose...

item.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
item.description: Is it the Department of Defense or the Department of War? The Gulf of Mexico or the Gulf of America? A vaccine—or an “individualized neoantigen treatment”? That’s the Trump-era vocabulary paradox...

item.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.description: This is it. Tonight is your last chance to lock in savings of up to $500 for your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass. These discounts end at 11:59 p.m. PT.

item.title: GlassWorm Campaign Uses Zig Dropper to Infect Multiple Developer IDEs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged yet another evolution of the ongoing GlassWorm campaign, which employs a new Zig dropper that's designed to stealthily infect all integrated development...

item.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Constellations —Constellations is a short story by Jeff...
